title: Melodorum oligocarpum
taxonomy: Melodorum oligocarpum is classified within the family Annonaceae. This family is commonly known as the custard apple family. It belongs to the order Magnoliales, which is now generally considered to be a part of the order Magnoliaceae in some classifications, although some sources still consider it separate. The order Magnoliaceae is a group of flowering plants that are ancient and often considered to be among the most primitive of flowering plants. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
